subtasks.description: Query AWS contract data to retrieve committed contract levels (RIs, Savings Plans) by service, term, and commitment amount
Query AWS spend data to retrieve actual monthly/period spend by service, filtering for usage covered by commitments

Reconcile contracted commitment levels against actual spend data; calculate variance (overcommitment vs underutilization); classify commitment status (healthy, over-committed, under-utilized) by service; generate recommendations for rebalancing or renewal

plan.cost_rationale: Two independent sql_gen queries (small tier) are sufficient—one for contracts and one for spend. Each is a straightforward single-source extraction with filtering and aggregation. The reconciliation is combined into one cross_recon (mid tier) that handles variance calculation, posture classification (over-committed vs under-utilized), and generates actionable recommendations. No separate policy_action is needed—classification and recommendation are integral to the reconciliation task. Mid tier is assigned because cross_recon must correlate two data sources, compute variance metrics, and apply multi-signal classification logic. Total: 3 subtasks, minimal and sufficient.

output.sql: SELECT i.contract_id, i.vendor_name, c.product AS service, SUM(i.amount_usd) AS committed_amount, COALESCE(SUM(c.amount_usd), 0) AS actual_spend, SUM(i.amount_usd) - COALESCE(SUM(c.amount_usd), 0) AS variance, CASE WHEN COALESCE(SUM(c.amount_usd), 0) > SUM(i.amount_usd) THEN 'Overcommitted' WHEN COALESCE(SUM(c.amount_usd), 0) < SUM(i.amount_usd) * 0.8 THEN 'Underutilized' ELSE 'On Track' END AS utilization_status FROM invoice_facts i LEFT JOIN consumption_facts c ON i.vendor_name = c.publisher WHERE i.vendor_name ILIKE '%AWS%' AND i.invoice_date >= '2024-01-01' GROUP BY i.contract_id, i.vendor_name, c.product ORDER BY committed_amount DESC

SELECT product AS service, DATE_TRUNC('month', event_date) AS spend_month, SUM(amount_usd) AS actual_spend FROM consumption_facts WHERE publisher = 'AWS' AND event_type = 'commit_covered' AND event_date >= '2024-01-01' GROUP BY product, DATE_TRUNC('month', event_date) ORDER BY spend_month, service
